title: Sync manifest — single source of truth replacing build.js OWNERSHIP, workspace-sync.md NEVER_PUSH/smart_copy enumeration, and CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules duplication
do_not_implement: Operator authorizes implementation in a separate step. This PROP creates monitor/sync-manifest.json schema and migration design ONLY — no manifest file, no workspace-sync.md edit, no build.js edit, no CLAUDE.md edit performed by this PROP-authoring run.
summary: Operator chose Option B (sync manifest) over PROP-017's targeted fix because the audit surfaced 15 gaps where 1 was visible. Three-way duplication across build.js OWNERSHIP, workspace-sync.md NEVER_PUSH+smart_copy enumeration, and CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules is the structural bug class — every new agent write path is a 3-file edit, and missing the workspace-sync edit is silent. This PROP designs a single manifest at monitor/sync-manifest.json that both consumers (workspace-sync FUSE→git push, build.js publish git→FUSE copy) read at runtime; a node script (monitor/scripts/sync-from-manifest.js) replaces the inlined smart_copy/sync_glob iteration; an integrity lint catches new fs.writeFileSync paths absent from the manifest within 24h (Option C folded in). Migration is 11 steps, each independently reversible; legacy enumeration mode is gated by env var for one rollout cycle. After validation, CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules and build.js OWNERSHIP become derived/auto-rendered views of the manifest, eliminating the failure mode entirely.

starting_inputs_from_prop_017.audit_findings_carried_forward: PROP-017's gap_audit section is the canonical input. 15 confirmed-active-writer gap files (now patched in workspace-sync.md as the targeted-fix interim), 17 covered smart_copy paths, 16 covered sync_glob paths, 13 NEVER_PUSH entries (inclusive of dynamic monitor/prompts/*.md rule), 15+ relic files in monitor/analyst/, monitor/decisions/, monitor/curmudgeon/. Manifest must cover all of these.
starting_inputs_from_prop_017.targeted_fix_already_shipped: Note: as of 2026-05-05T19:30Z, monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md already contains the PROP-017 13 smart_copy + 2 sync_glob gap fills (lines 197-201, 211-213, 225-231, 269-271, 277-279, with `# PROP-017 gap fills (2026-05-05)` comments). This PROP supersedes that interim by replacing the entire enumeration with a manifest-driven loader. The targeted-fix lines are NOT to be removed yet — they keep the stale-loop closed during the manifest rollout window. Operator removes them at migration step 8 once the manifest path is verified.
starting_inputs_from_prop_017.sync_glob_recursion_decision: Per PROP-017 audit note: existing sync_glob is intentionally non-recursive (`for f in ${WORKSPACE}/${rel}/${pat}` with no **/ pattern). Manifest preserves this — globs are non-recursive by default. A `recursive: true` boolean on the manifest entry can opt in if needed later, but no current entry needs it.

top_level_shape.generated_by: operator | tinker | <agent-id> — last writer for audit

path.description: Repo-relative path. Literal file (e.g. 'data/wins.json') or non-recursive glob (e.g. 'monitor/changes/*.json'). Globs use shell-style * and ?. Recursive ** is FORBIDDEN — use a separate entry per directory tier instead, or set recursive:true (see below).
path.validation: Path must not start with '/' or '..'; must not contain '**' unless recursive:true is also set.

direction.description: Authoritative direction across the FUSE↔git boundary. Implementations interpret as: workspace-sync (FUSE→git) treats workspace_to_clone|both as push-eligible (smart_copy with mtime guard); build.js publish (git→FUSE) treats clone_to_workspace|both as copy on publish. `never` means neither consumer touches the file — used for clone-internal generated files (workspace-sync-skips.jsonl is special-cased separately as clone_to_workspace because integrity reads it from FUSE) and for FUSE-only relics (with classification:'scratch').

owner_agent.description: Diagnostic / documentation only. The agent or pipeline component that primarily authors this file. 'multi-writer' for files with intentional multiple writers (e.g. tracker.json — decider + curmudgeon).

classification.description: Semantic category. data=canonical project data (wins.json), ledger=trackers and processed lists (open-issues.json, processed-reviews.json), summary=human-facing latest-*.txt rollups (latest-review-summary, alerts.txt, morning-briefing.txt), scratch=relics and forensic one-offs (FUSE-only by design), source=code/prompts/build scripts (git-authoritative), docs=generated HTML/machine-readable layer (built artifacts), flag=presence-vs-absence semantic markers (prop-009-enforce.flag), log=append-only logs (workspace-sync-skips.jsonl), report=append-only per-run / per-ID artifacts (daily-report-*.json, run-*.json, EXP-*.json, WIN-*.json, PROP-*.json).

notes.description: Free-form prose: rationale, gotchas, link to PROPs/HNOTEs that established this entry. Not machine-parsed.

added_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when this entry was added or last materially changed.

added_by.description: Who authored this entry. Operator is normal for hand-curated entries; agent name when an automated migration tool wrote it (rare).

recursive.description: Opt-in flag for recursive glob expansion. When true, path may contain ** and the loader walks recursively. When false (default), glob is non-recursive (matches direct children only). Currently no entry needs recursive:true — all existing patterns expand only one directory level.

supersedes.description: If this entry replaces a prior pattern (e.g. 'monitor/decisions/suggested-patches.json' relic superseded by 'monitor/decisions/suggested-patches-*.json' glob), name the prior entry path. Diagnostic only.

deprecation_at.description: ISO timestamp when this entry should be re-evaluated for removal (relic candidates that may be cleaned up later).

validation_rules: (V1) Each entry's `path` must be unique within the entries array (literal entries only).

(V2) Glob entries may overlap a literal entry only when the literal entry comes first in the array — first-match-wins for the loader. Validator emits a warning if literal-after-glob ordering would cause the glob to mask the literal.
(V3) Two glob entries that match the same path are an error — the validator must flag overlapping globs.
(V4) `direction` must be one of the four enum values; case-sensitive.
(V5) `path` must be repo-relative: must not start with '/', must not contain '..', must not contain '**' unless `recursive:true` is set.
(V6) `added_at` must be a valid ISO-8601 UTC string with 'Z' suffix.
(V7) `classification` must be one of the nine enum values; case-sensitive.
(V8) `recursive:true` requires `path` to contain at least one '**' segment (otherwise the flag is meaningless).

(V9) The manifest itself must be classified as direction:'clone_to_workspace' classification:'source' (operator-authoritative; pushed git→FUSE on publish; never pushed FUSE→git). Missing self-entry is a fatal validation error.

(V10) Cross-check: every entry path must either resolve to an existing file in repo HEAD (literal) OR have at least one matching file (glob) — orphan entries flagged as warnings (not errors, since intentional deprecation_at entries may have zero matches).

example_entries_per_classification.notes: Canonical WIN data. Decider commits from clone (clone_to_workspace via build.js publish); universal-pusher rescues FUSE→git on 403 (workspace_to_clone via smart_copy mtime guard). Both directions live under the `both` umbrella.
PROP-017 primary case. Decider writes from clone; universal-pusher copies to FUSE on 403; workspace-sync round-trips back. Was the staleness-loop trigger before PROP-017 targeted fix landed.
Curmudgeon writes to FUSE per curmudgeon.md line 354. Decider-intake reads to spot critical/major holes. Was a PROP-017 confirmed gap before targeted fix landed.
One-off forensic audit file from 2026-04 round. No active writer. Documented as FUSE-only-by-design so flat-glob iteration would not force-sync.
Build pipeline source. Git-authoritative; FUSE copy is read-only (would-be edits get rescued by NEVER_PUSH strand mechanism per PROP-013).
Generated artifact. Built in clone via `node build.js html`; copied to FUSE on publish. workspace-sync explicitly refuses to push (legacy NEVER_PUSH semantics preserved).
PROP-009r2 C-1: presence = enforce mode, absence = shadow mode. Content irrelevant; only the file's existence matters. Operator toggles via `git rm` / `touch`.

Append-only log written inside the clone via fs.appendFileSync. Pushed via normal `git add -A monitor/`. clone_to_workspace direction needed so structure-integrity (FUSE-side reader) can see fresh skip records on publish.
Append-only per-run reports. Glob is non-recursive (single directory level).

The manifest itself. Operator-authoritative via git clone. workspace-sync MUST refuse to push this — agent-side mutations are the ouroboros problem (a pipeline that edits its own contract). PROPs author proposed manifest changes; operator merges via standard PR/commit flow.

schema_design.validation_script: monitor/scripts/validate-sync-manifest.js — invoked by sync-from-manifest.js on every workspace-sync run before iteration begins; invoked by build.js on every publish before sync-to-workspace; invoked by lint-sync-manifest.js (Option C) on every integrity run. Failures: V1, V3, V4, V5, V6, V7, V8, V9 are fatal (script exits non-zero, calling consumer aborts). V2, V10 are warnings (logged, calling consumer continues).

workspace_sync_md_step_2_rewrite.before_outline: Step 2 currently inlines: NEVER_PUSH array (line 59-82), is_never_push() function with 6 self-tests, smart_copy() helper with mtime guard, sync_glob() helper, then ~70 lines of explicit smart_copy and sync_glob invocations covering analyst / curmudgeon / poller / decider / data-rescue / docs/assets / social / integrity / tinker / status. PROP-017 targeted-fix added 13 smart_copy lines + 2 sync_glob lines. Total Step 2 length: ~210 lines including comments.
workspace_sync_md_step_2_rewrite.after_outline: Step 2 calls one node script that consumes monitor/sync-manifest.json and performs all FUSE→clone copies, populating SKIP_LOG and NEVER_PUSH_STRANDS exactly as the inlined helpers did. workspace-sync.md retains: SKIP_LOG mktemp setup, smart_copy/sync_glob helpers as bash functions (kept for legibility — the script writes pseudo-bash invocations to a side channel and bash executes them), Step 3 (regeneration), Step 3.5 (strand rescue), Step 3.6 (verifier + audit), Step 4 (commit/push), Step 4b (run report). The reduction is concentrated in Step 2: ~210 lines → ~60 lines (helpers retained, but the explicit per-file enumeration is replaced by a 30-line manifest-iteration loop).

kept: smart_copy() bash helper (lines 139-173) — script invokes this via shell out, OR the script implements its own copy and writes the skip log directly
NEVER_PUSH_STRANDS associative array (line 137) — script populates this via a side-channel JSON file that bash reads at end of Step 2
added: After the script returns, bash reads /tmp/strands-${RUN_ID}.json and populates NEVER_PUSH_STRANDS for Step 3.5
Self-test of the manifest schema at start of Step 2 (validate-sync-manifest.js exit-non-zero aborts the run)

never_push_semantics_preservation.approach: NEVER_PUSH-equivalent semantics are encoded via direction. workspace-sync's effective deny-list is: any entry whose direction is clone_to_workspace OR never. The sync-from-manifest.js loader filters: `if (entry.direction === 'workspace_to_clone' || entry.direction === 'both') push_eligible(entry)`. Strand detection (PROP-013) still fires when a clone_to_workspace-classified file has FUSE-newer content (decider patched a source file; push 403'd). The bash `is_never_push()` helper can be retained as a fallback safety check (it would now read the manifest), but the sync-from-manifest.js script's filter is the primary enforcement.
never_push_semantics_preservation.dynamic_rule_for_prompts: Current dynamic rule `case "$dst" in monitor/prompts/*.md) return 0 ;; esac` becomes a manifest entry with path='monitor/prompts/*.md' direction='clone_to_workspace' classification='source' notes='Dynamic rule covers all .md files under monitor/prompts/ at any depth; equivalent to the prior bash case statement.' Or, if non-recursive globs are insufficient, recursive:true with path='monitor/prompts/**/*.md'.

build_js_OWNERSHIP_object_disposition.approach: Replace the OWNERSHIP literal (lines 58-127) with `const OWNERSHIP = loadManifestAsOwnership('monitor/sync-manifest.json');` where loadManifestAsOwnership is a small function that reads the manifest, maps each direction to build.js's 4-category vocabulary (git | workspace | append_only | append_only_glob), and returns the same shape the rest of build.js expects. The mapping is: clone_to_workspace → 'git', workspace_to_clone → 'workspace', both → 'git' (build.js's git→FUSE direction is the same; workspace-sync's FUSE→git rescue is invisible to build.js), never (with classification!='scratch') → omitted (build.js does nothing), never (classification='scratch') → omitted (relic), report classification → 'append_only_glob' (per existing semantics for monitor/decisions/ etc.), per-ID classification (proposal/report under monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ etc.) → 'append_only'.
build_js_OWNERSHIP_object_disposition.checkDirectionViolations_compatibility: build.js's checkDirectionViolations (lines 195-216) reads OWNERSHIP['workspace'] entries. After mapping, workspace_to_clone manifest entries appear there exactly as before. No behavior change.
build_js_OWNERSHIP_object_disposition.alternative_full_replacement: If reading the manifest at build.js boot is fragile (e.g. JSON parse failure), keep a STATIC fallback OWNERSHIP object compiled from the manifest at PROP-018 commit time, with a comment 'GENERATED FROM monitor/sync-manifest.json — DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. Run `node monitor/scripts/render-build-ownership.js > /tmp/snippet && operator pastes`. Auto-regenerated by integrity if drift detected.' This is more conservative — drift between manifest and build.js OWNERSHIP becomes a soft-complaint integrity finding, not a runtime crash.
build_js_OWNERSHIP_object_disposition.lean: Run-time load with a static fallback. If JSON.parse(manifest) throws or validation fails, build.js logs the error and falls back to the static OWNERSHIP that was the last known-good. Integrity flags the drift on its next run.

claude_md_file_ownership_rules_dispositi.approach: Convert the prose section (CLAUDE.md lines 77-119) to a derived/auto-rendered summary. The section keeps the prose preamble and the additive-edit exceptions (PROP-009, DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE), but the four sub-tables (git-owned / workspace-owned / append-only / unclassified) become a single 'See monitor/sync-manifest.json' pointer with a one-paragraph rendered summary that monitor/scripts/render-ownership-summary.js produces.
claude_md_file_ownership_rules_dispositi.auto_render_workflow: On every operator commit that touches monitor/sync-manifest.json, a build hook (TBD: pre-commit or via CI) runs render-ownership-summary.js and updates CLAUDE.md. Integrity flags drift between manifest and CLAUDE.md prose as a moderate finding. Or: skip the auto-render and just add the pointer; CLAUDE.md prose becomes a brief 1-paragraph summary that the operator updates manually when editing the manifest.
claude_md_file_ownership_rules_dispositi.lean: Manual prose summary + integrity drift check. Auto-render is over-engineering for a section that changes monthly at most. The integrity lint catches drift; operator updates when prompted.

lint_check.scope: Walk all agent prompts (monitor/prompts/*.md, recursive into reference/) AND build-scripts/*.js AND build.js. For each file, find write targets via regex matching: `fs.writeFileSync\(`, `fs.appendFileSync\(`, `fs.copyFileSync\(`, bash `>` redirects to repo paths, bash `cp` invocations, the smart_copy helper. Extract the destination path. Cross-check every destination against the manifest. Emit a finding for any path that is not present in the manifest (literal match OR glob match).
lint_check.false_positive_handling: Write targets that are obviously transient (`/tmp/...`, `mktemp` outputs, `process.cwd()`-relative ephemeral files) are excluded via a hard-coded prefix list in the linter: '/tmp/', '${RUN_ID}', '${SESSION}/', etc. Write targets that are dynamic at runtime (e.g. `path.join(workspace, dynVar)`) are flagged as `dynamic-target`, NOT `missing-manifest-entry`, so they get manual review without firing the regression alert.

where_should_lint_live.argument_for_integrity: Integrity runs daily on Haiku (cheap), already runs structural checks (broken links, build drift, data-prose consistency). Adding manifest-coverage to its remit fits the agent's name. Daily cadence catches drift within 24h — adequate. Output goes to integrity/alerts.txt and report-*.json, where decider-intake already reads them. No new agent or new schedule needed.
where_should_lint_live.argument_for_tinker: Tinker is the architectural agent — the manifest IS architecture. Tinker can do deeper analysis (root cause: was the prompt edit a one-off or part of a pattern?), can author PROPs to extend the manifest, can correlate with previous reports. But tinker runs daily-or-less (Opus, expensive); the lint is a 30-second cheap check that doesn't need Opus reasoning.
where_should_lint_live.lean: BOTH, in tiers. Integrity runs the cheap lint daily as a soft-complaint (logged to integrity/alerts.txt + report-*.json). Tinker's morning soft-complaints grep picks up the alert; if the same finding persists 3+ days, tinker escalates with root-cause analysis (Mode 4) and authors a PROP to either extend the manifest OR fix the prompt/script that introduced the rogue write. This separates detection (cheap, daily) from remediation (expensive, judgment).
where_should_lint_live.concrete_assignment: structure-integrity.md gets a new step (likely Section 7e or similar) that calls `node monitor/scripts/lint-sync-manifest.js` and appends findings to integrity/alerts.txt with prefix `[manifest-lint]`. tinker.md Mode 4 reference (tinker-proposals-and-fixes.md) gets a new check: 'If integrity has flagged manifest-lint findings persisting >3 runs, author a PROP to remediate.'

option_c_integration.scope: Walk the FUSE workspace; for each non-trivial agent-written file, classify it against the manifest. Emit findings for: (a) FUSE-mtime-newer-than-main on a clone_to_workspace entry (direction violation), (b) FUSE files with no manifest entry (gap candidate), (c) FUSE files matching a never-direction or scratch-classification entry but appearing in workspace-sync commits (force-sync of intended-FUSE-only).

severity_rubric.manifest-gap-candidate: moderate. Same severity as a schema regression. Action: tinker authors PROP to extend manifest OR scripts/prompts that introduced the rogue write are fixed.
severity_rubric.direction-violation: moderate. Same severity as PROP-013 NEVER_PUSH strand. Action: PROP-013 strand rescue (already implemented) handles the recovery; integrity finding documents the drift.
severity_rubric.relic-deprecation-due: low / informational. Operator decides whether to delete the file or extend the deprecation_at.
severity_rubric.orphan-manifest-entry: low. A manifest entry whose path resolves to no actual file. Either the file was deleted intentionally (entry should be removed) or there's a typo. Validator V10 emits this.

option_c_integration.integration_with_workspace_sync_audit: Option C's gap detector and the existing PROP-017 audit live in the same conceptual space. After PROP-018 lands, the periodic FUSE-vs-main staleness audit recommended in PROP-017 (`measurement_plan.follow_up_after_resolution`) becomes redundant — the integrity lint subsumes it. tinker can drop that audit from Mode 3 cost engineering.
option_c_integration.exemption_handling: Some files legitimately have no manifest entry (truly transient .tmp- scratch). These are excluded via the `excludePatterns` list in lint-sync-manifest.js. The exclusion list is short (current: .tmp-*, __test_*, node_modules/, .git/). New exclusion patterns are operator-authored via PROP — agents may not extend the exclusion list autonomously.

relic_strategy.principle: Each relic file gets an explicit manifest entry with direction='never' and classification='scratch'. This makes the FUSE-only-by-design intent visible — adding a flat-glob iteration would force-sync them, and the manifest entries document why that would be wrong.

relic_catalog_per_prop_017.rationale: No active writer since 2026-04-06. Probably superseded by daily-report-*.json glob. Documented as relic.
Non-timestamped variant; superseded by suggested-patches-*.json glob. Operator may delete safely after deprecation_at.
One-off PROP work file. Should not have a writer; if a writer reappears, that's a regression.
One-off backup of tracker.json from a 2026-04-05 round. Safe to delete.
One-off forensic notes. Was relevant for WIN-004 investigation; archived in spirit.
External design review HTML. One-off; not part of pipeline.
Debug scratch. Excluded by linter prefix .tmp-*. Manifest entry retained for documentation; loader treats as 'never' direction.
FUSE write-test marker. Excluded by linter prefix __test_*.

relic_strategy.relic_cleanup_optional_separate_pass: PROP-018 does NOT delete any relic file. The manifest documents them; operator decides whether to delete (via direct `git rm` from clone) at any deprecation_at boundary. The integrity lint flags relic-deprecation-due as a low/informational finding — operator notices in morning briefing.

migration_steps: STEP 1: Author monitor/sync-manifest.json with skeleton (schema_version, generated_by, last_modified, comment, entries:[]). Operator commits to clone, pushes. Reversible: `git revert`.

STEP 2: Migrate explicit smart_copy entries from workspace-sync.md lines 188-285 into manifest entries with direction='workspace_to_clone' or 'both'. Each line → one entry. Set added_by='operator' on all. Operator commits, pushes. Reversible: `git revert`.
STEP 3: Migrate sync_glob entries (16 globs per PROP-017 audit) into manifest entries with direction='workspace_to_clone'. Glob path stays non-recursive. Reversible: `git revert`.

STEP 4: Migrate the 15 PROP-017 confirmed-gap entries that workspace-sync.md added 2026-05-05 as targeted-fix lines (already merged with `# PROP-017 gap fills` comments). These get manifest entries identical to Step 2/3 entries (smart_copy → workspace_to_clone, sync_glob → workspace_to_clone). Reversible: `git revert`.

STEP 5: Migrate NEVER_PUSH array entries into manifest with direction='clone_to_workspace' (for source/docs/flag classifications) or direction='never' (for clone-internal generated files like pending-digest.json — note: workspace-sync-skips.jsonl is direction='clone_to_workspace' per build.js OWNERSHIP rationale). Reversible: `git revert`.
STEP 6: Add relic entries from PROP-017's relic catalog with direction='never' classification='scratch' and deprecation_at. ~17 entries. Reversible: `git revert`.

STEP 7: Author monitor/scripts/sync-from-manifest.js — node script that loads manifest, validates (V1-V10), iterates entries, performs FUSE→clone copies via embedded smart_copy logic (re-implementing the bash helper in node), populates SKIP_LOG and writes a STRANDS_OUT JSON file. Author monitor/scripts/validate-sync-manifest.js (loaded by the sync script and standalone via build.js / lint script). Author monitor/scripts/lint-sync-manifest.js (Option C integrity check). Author monitor/scripts/render-ownership-summary.js (CLAUDE.md prose helper, optional). Operator commits, pushes. Reversible: `git revert` of the scripts directory.

STEP 8: Rewrite monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md Step 2 to invoke sync-from-manifest.js (replacing the inlined NEVER_PUSH array, is_never_push() function, smart_copy invocations, sync_glob invocations). KEEP the bash smart_copy() and sync_glob() helpers — the script invokes them OR re-implements them in node; either way, the helpers are not removed (preserves backward-compat if --legacy-fallback is set). Also REMOVE the 15 PROP-017 targeted-fix lines added 2026-05-05; those are now manifest-driven. Operator commits, pushes. Reversible: `git revert` of workspace-sync.md commit. Risk: if first invocation has a bug, workspace-sync runs but fails to copy any files — caught by Step 4b run report (zero files committed). Mitigation: manual revert + re-attempt.

STEP 9: Update build.js OWNERSHIP to load from manifest (per workspace_sync_md_step_2_rewrite.build_js_OWNERSHIP_object_disposition.lean = run-time load with static fallback). The static fallback OWNERSHIP literal is generated at PROP-018-implementation-time from the manifest (operator runs `node monitor/scripts/render-build-ownership.js > /tmp/snippet`, pastes into build.js). Run-time load tries to read the manifest; on parse failure, falls back to static. Reversible: `git revert`.

STEP 10: Update CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules section (lines 77-119) to reference monitor/sync-manifest.json as the canonical source. Replace the four sub-tables with a single one-paragraph rendered summary plus a pointer 'See monitor/sync-manifest.json for the canonical file inventory and direction rules.' KEEP the additive-edit exceptions (PROP-009, DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE) as prose — those are policy, not file-listing. KEEP the Human Notes Rule, possibly relaxed (see side_effect_dual_write_rule below). Reversible: `git revert`.

STEP 11: Update monitor/prompts/structure-integrity.md to add a new step (Section 7e or similar) calling `node monitor/scripts/lint-sync-manifest.js` and appending findings to monitor/integrity/alerts.txt with prefix `[manifest-lint]`. Update monitor/prompts/reference/tinker-proposals-and-fixes.md to add a check: 'If integrity has flagged manifest-lint findings persisting >3 runs, author a PROP to remediate.' Reversible: `git revert`. After 7 days clean, remove the legacy fallback in sync-from-manifest.js (commit with message 'PROP-018 cleanup: remove legacy enumeration fallback').

STEP 12 (parity test, optional): Before STEP 8 ships, run a parity test on a synthetic workload: (a) snapshot the FUSE workspace state, (b) run the OLD enumeration-driven workspace-sync, capture commit set, (c) reset, (d) run the NEW manifest-driven sync-from-manifest.js, capture commit set, (e) diff. The two commit sets should be identical (or differ only in newly-listed gap files which were never copied before).
migration_plan.ordering_rationale: Steps 1-6 are pure manifest authoring (no consumer changes). Steps 7 is script authoring (no consumer changes). Step 8 is the cutover for workspace-sync (highest-risk single step). Step 9 is the cutover for build.js (lower-risk; static fallback protects against runtime failures). Steps 10-11 are documentation and observability. Step 12 (parity test) is recommended before Step 8 cutover — operator runs in a sandbox clone, not main.

measurement_plan.primary_metric: Number of new manifest gaps detected over 30 days post-rollout. Target: zero. Disprove: any non-zero count where the gap was caused by a new agent write path that should have been added during the prompt edit.
secondary_metrics: Manifest validation passes on every workspace-sync run (V1-V9 fatal checks). Target: 100%.
build.js publish proceeds with static-fallback OWNERSHIP at most 0 times per week (i.e. manifest load succeeds every time). Soft target: 0 fallback events; hard target: <2 per week.
Integrity lint finds zero `manifest-gap-candidate` or `direction-violation` findings on FUSE files >24h old.
Stale-loop incidents (PROP-017 root symptom: decider re-processing already-processed reviews): zero per week post-rollout.
disprove_conditions: Any agent-written FUSE file appears in main without a manifest entry — the manifest didn't catch the gap.
Any manifest entry references a non-existent file (orphan) for >7 days — entry maintenance is failing.
Manifest validation fails on a workspace-sync run AND the run still attempts to copy files (validator should abort; if it doesn't, the script is broken).
build.js OWNERSHIP run-time load fails AND static fallback also fails — pipeline is wedged.
Parity test (Step 12) shows the manifest-driven path produces a commit set DIFFERENT from the legacy enumeration on identical input — migration is incorrect.
Same-bug-class gap (someone adds a write path, manifest misses it) recurs within 30d post-rollout — manifest didn't eliminate the bug class.
measurement_plan.monitoring_window: 30 days post-rollout. After 30 days clean (zero gap-candidates, zero direction-violations >24h-stale, zero stale-loops), declare resolved.
follow_up_after_resolution: Remove legacy fallback path in sync-from-manifest.js (commit at day 30+).
Drop tinker's periodic FUSE-vs-main staleness audit (Mode 3) — subsumed by integrity lint.
Operator schedules a quarterly relic-deprecation review (look for relic entries past their deprecation_at and decide on cleanup).

side_effect_dual_write_rule.current_rule: CLAUDE.md Human Notes Rule: 'All `human-notes.json` files (`monitor/analyst/`, `monitor/decisions/`, `monitor/social/`, `monitor/curmudgeon/`) are dual-write. When adding a human note, ALWAYS write to BOTH the FUSE workspace AND the git clone, then commit+push from the clone.'
side_effect_dual_write_rule.rule_origin: Workaround for the gap that workspace-sync didn't round-trip 3 of 4 human-notes.json files (only monitor/analyst/human-notes.json was explicitly synced).
side_effect_dual_write_rule.post_prop_018_state: All 4 human-notes.json files have manifest entries with direction='both' (or 'workspace_to_clone'). workspace-sync round-trips them within 4h (one cycle). The workaround is no longer required for correctness.
options: OPTION A — DROP the dual-write rule entirely. Notes go to FUSE; workspace-sync round-trips within 4h.

OPTION B — KEEP the dual-write rule for urgent notes; document as 'optional belt-and-suspenders for notes that need to reach clone-reading agents (decider, curmudgeon) before next workspace-sync cycle (~4h)'.
OPTION C — Tier the rule by note type: routine notes single-write to FUSE; urgent notes dual-write.
side_effect_dual_write_rule.argument_for_A: Cleaner mental model. The manifest is the single source of truth; the dual-write was a workaround for missing manifest coverage. Workspace-sync runs every 4h — that's the contract. Operator/agent communication is synchronous within 4h, asynchronous beyond. This is acceptable for human notes which are intrinsically not-real-time.
side_effect_dual_write_rule.argument_for_B: 4h is a real delay for some notes. Operator may want to pause an agent's behavior immediately; if the note has to wait 4h to reach clone-reading decider, the agent may take a destructive action in the interim. Dual-write keeps the override path open. Cost is low: humans already remember to dual-write today.
side_effect_dual_write_rule.argument_for_C: Mid-ground: routine notes don't need the cost; urgent notes get the protection. But the cognitive overhead of deciding 'is this urgent?' on every note is non-trivial.
side_effect_dual_write_rule.lean: OPTION B. Keep the dual-write rule but reframe as optional/recommended-for-urgent. The 4h cycle is good enough for 95% of notes; the 5% that need faster propagation justify keeping the option open. Cost of keeping the rule: zero (humans already do it). Cost of dropping it: occasional 4h delay where operator-induced agent change should have been immediate. The risk asymmetry favors keeping it.
side_effect_dual_write_rule.claude_md_prose_change: Update the Human Notes Rule paragraph to: 'All `human-notes.json` files have manifest entries with direction `both` or `workspace_to_clone`; workspace-sync round-trips them within 4h. For URGENT notes that need to reach clone-reading agents (decider, curmudgeon) before next workspace-sync cycle, dual-write to FUSE AND git clone, then commit+push from the clone. For routine notes, single-write to FUSE is sufficient.'

Q1_manifest_location.question: Should the manifest live at monitor/sync-manifest.json or workspace-manifest.json or somewhere else?

options_considered.pros: Lives alongside other monitor/ subdirs; workspace-sync's domain

options_considered.cons: Slightly misleading — it's not a workspace inventory, it's a sync contract

options_considered.pros: data/ is a known top-level dir
options_considered.cons: Conflates project data with pipeline plumbing
Conflicts with package.json conceptually; too generic

options_considered.pros: Lives with the prompts that consume it

Q1_manifest_location.decision: monitor/sync-manifest.json. It's a workspace-sync concern, fits alongside monitor/curmudgeon/, monitor/analyst/, etc. Names what it does (sync manifest) without overclaiming (it's not a 'workspace inventory' — it's a sync contract).
Q1_manifest_location.tradeoff: Slightly less discoverable for new readers than a root-level file. Mitigated by CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules pointing at it explicitly.

Q2_never_push_concept_disposition.question: Should NEVER_PUSH stay as a separate concept in workspace-sync.md or get folded into the manifest as direction='never'?

options_considered.name: Keep separate
options_considered.cons: Re-introduces the duplication problem PROP-018 is solving

options_considered.name: Fold into manifest as direction='clone_to_workspace' or 'never'
options_considered.pros: Single source of truth; eliminates duplication
options_considered.cons: Slightly less obvious for readers who scanned NEVER_PUSH and now have to scan manifest

Q2_never_push_concept_disposition.decision: Fold into manifest. NEVER_PUSH semantics are encoded as direction values: clone_to_workspace (build.js publish copies git→FUSE; workspace-sync refuses to push) for source/docs/flag/log files, and direction='never' for clone-internal-only files. The bash is_never_push() helper can stay in workspace-sync.md as a fallback safety check that reads the manifest and applies the same logic — this is a defensive guard, not a separate registry.
Q2_never_push_concept_disposition.tradeoff: Readers who used to grep workspace-sync.md for NEVER_PUSH must now grep the manifest. The PROP rewrites workspace-sync.md to call out the manifest path explicitly, mitigating the discoverability cost.

Q3_path_uniqueness_and_overlap.question: Should the manifest enforce uniqueness on path? What about overlapping globs?
Q3_path_uniqueness_and_overlap.decision: Yes, enforce path uniqueness on literal entries (V1). Glob entries: enforced via first-match-wins when the loader iterates; validator emits a warning (V2) when literal-after-glob ordering is detected (because the literal will be masked); validator emits an error (V3) when two glob entries match the same path. Concrete rules: literal entries are matched first (exact path comparison), glob entries are matched in array order if no literal matches.
Q3_path_uniqueness_and_overlap.tradeoff: Strict ordering rules add cognitive load when authoring entries. Mitigated by the validator: violations are flagged loudly with specific overlap descriptions.

Q4_who_can_edit_the_manifest.question: Should the manifest be in NEVER_PUSH itself (operator-edited only) or eligible to be edited by agents?

options_considered.name: Operator-only
options_considered.pros: Avoids ouroboros (an agent editing its own contract)
options_considered.cons: Slows down genuine pipeline evolution — every new write path requires operator-mediated PROP review

options_considered.name: Agent-eligible (with constraints)
options_considered.cons: Pipeline-edits-pipeline failure mode; an agent bug propagates through the manifest to all consumers

options_considered.name: Operator-only with structured PROP-driven extensions
options_considered.pros: Best of both — agents PROPOSE manifest changes; operator commits

Q4_who_can_edit_the_manifest.decision: Operator-only (the manifest's manifest-entry has direction='clone_to_workspace'; workspace-sync refuses to push). Agents propose extensions via PROPs that operator merges. The workflow: integrity lint detects a missing-manifest-entry, tinker (Mode 4) authors a PROP to extend the manifest, operator reviews and commits.
Q4_who_can_edit_the_manifest.rationale: The manifest is pipeline policy. Letting agents self-edit policy is the failure mode that justifies having the manifest in the first place. Operator-only ownership keeps the change rate sane (~1-3 entries per week max) and the audit trail clear (every manifest commit is human-mediated).
Q4_who_can_edit_the_manifest.tradeoff: Adds 1-3 day latency for genuine new write paths. Operator has the option to fast-track via direct commit if a write path is operationally urgent. The integrity lint flags missing entries within 24h, so the worst case is: agent introduces new write path → integrity lint flags it next morning → tinker authors PROP → operator commits next day. ~48h max latency, zero unbounded gaps.

Q5_rollback_story.question: What's the rollback story if the manifest-driven workspace-sync introduces a regression?

options_considered.name: Keep old enumeration commented out for one rollout cycle
options_considered.cons: Bloats workspace-sync.md by ~150 lines; the 'commented-out fallback' becomes drift bait

options_considered.name: Env-var gated legacy mode in sync-from-manifest.js
options_considered.pros: Cleaner; rollback is one env var change
options_considered.cons: Requires the script to actually maintain a legacy code path internally

options_considered.name: Tag the pre-cutover commit as `pre-prop-018-rollback` for `git revert`
options_considered.cons: Rollback is a multi-file revert (workspace-sync.md, build.js, CLAUDE.md, structure-integrity.md); harder to do cleanly under time pressure

Q5_rollback_story.decision: Env-var gated legacy mode in sync-from-manifest.js for one rollout cycle (7 days). The script supports `LEGACY_MODE=1` env var that disables manifest iteration and invokes the bash helpers (smart_copy, sync_glob) with the same enumeration as pre-PROP-018. After 7 days clean, remove the legacy mode in a separate cleanup commit.
Q5_rollback_story.rationale: Cleanest rollback path: operator sets LEGACY_MODE=1 in workspace-sync's task config, and the next workspace-sync run uses the old behavior — no git revert needed, no clone confusion. The 7-day window matches the monitoring_window for early regression detection.
Q5_rollback_story.tradeoff: Maintains two code paths in sync-from-manifest.js for one week. Mitigated by: (a) the legacy path is just a wrapper around the existing bash helpers, ~30 lines, (b) deleting it after 7 days is a single-commit cleanup.

non_goals_compliance.no_NEVER_PUSH_membership_changes: Confirmed. No file currently in NEVER_PUSH is being made push-eligible by this PROP. The migration preserves existing semantics; only the storage location and iteration mechanism change.
non_goals_compliance.no_digest_reviews_js_changes: Confirmed. digest-reviews.js's staleness mechanism is correct.
non_goals_compliance.no_universal_pusher_logic_changes: Confirmed. Decider's universal-pusher correctly copies files to FUSE; this PROP only addresses the FUSE→git return leg via the manifest's direction='both' classification.
non_goals_compliance.no_broader_architectural_cleanup: Confirmed. Scope is the smart_copy iteration → manifest migration. Agent prompt restructuring, CLAUDE.md prose rewrite (beyond File Ownership Rules section), and other architectural cleanups are out of scope.
non_goals_compliance.prop_017_preserved: Confirmed. PROP-017's targeted fix lines remain in workspace-sync.md until Step 8 cutover. PROP-017 status is updated to 'superseded-by-PROP-018' as a follow-up after PROP-018 lands; the file itself is not deleted (kept as alternative-not-chosen reference per directive non-goals).

completion_criteria_self_check.schema_concrete: Yes — schema_design.entry_shape lists 9 required-or-optional fields with types and validation. example_entries_per_classification has 10 example entries spanning all 9 classifications and 3 of 4 directions (workspace_to_clone, clone_to_workspace, both, never; the example for `never` direction covers scratch classification and clone-internal). validation_rules lists 10 rules.
completion_criteria_self_check.migration_steps_ordered: Yes — 11-step sequence in migration_plan.migration_steps, with step_dependencies graph and ordering_rationale. Each step independently reversible (git revert).
completion_criteria_self_check.option_c_folded: Yes — migration_plan.option_c_integration designs the integrity lint check, including primary_check_pseudocode, severity_rubric (4 finding kinds), and integration_with_workspace_sync_audit (subsumes prior tinker periodic audit).
completion_criteria_self_check.relic_strategy_clear: Yes — migration_plan.relic_strategy classifies all 17 relic files from PROP-017 catalog as direction='never' classification='scratch' with deprecation_at dates. Relic cleanup is operator-driven, separate pass.
completion_criteria_self_check.side_effects_addressed: Yes — side_effect_dual_write_rule argues OPTION B (keep as optional/recommended-for-urgent). build.js OWNERSHIP disposition argued in workspace_sync_md_step_2_rewrite.build_js_OWNERSHIP_object_disposition (run-time load with static fallback). CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules disposition argued in workspace_sync_md_step_2_rewrite.claude_md_file_ownership_rules_disposition (manual prose summary + integrity drift check).
completion_criteria_self_check.open_questions_addressed: Yes — all 5 open design questions (Q1-Q5) answered with options_considered, decision, rationale, and tradeoff in the open_questions_addressed section. Decisions: Q1 monitor/sync-manifest.json; Q2 fold into manifest; Q3 strict uniqueness with first-match-wins for globs; Q4 operator-only with PROP-driven agent proposals; Q5 env-var gated legacy mode for 7-day rollout cycle.
completion_criteria_self_check.directive_marked_completed: Pending — tinker will mark DIRECTIVE-20260505-002 status:'completed' in this same run per DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE additive-edit exception.

verification_pattern.purpose: Used by tinker's PROP Lifecycle Verification (Mode 1 / previous_followup) to decide if PROP-018 is FIXED or STILL_BROKEN.
verification_pattern.primary_test: After PROP-018 lands in full (all 11 migration steps + 7-day legacy-mode window): (a) curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/funwithscience-org/dome-model-review/main/monitor/sync-manifest.json — file exists with valid JSON; (b) `node monitor/scripts/validate-sync-manifest.js` — exits 0 (V1-V9 pass); (c) `node monitor/scripts/lint-sync-manifest.js` — exits 0 (zero manifest-gap-candidate findings); (d) workspace-sync's last 5 run reports show files_committed > 0 across the manifest-driven path.
verification_pattern.secondary_test: Compare workspace-sync's commit set 30 days pre-PROP-018 vs 30 days post-PROP-018: should be a superset (more gap files now covered) with zero new force-syncs of relic files. If a relic file appears in any post-PROP-018 commit, the manifest entry was misconfigured.
verification_pattern.still_broken_reason_template: Manifest absent or invalid: file=<path>, reason=<missing|parse_error|validation_failure>. Or: lint findings present: kind=<kind>, count=<n>. Or: workspace-sync legacy mode activated: env=LEGACY_MODE=1 set on <date>, indicates manifest-driven path failed.
